question: "Tripura Sundari Temple" is situated at ________
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: The "Tripura Sundari Temple" is situated in Udaipur (Option C), which is a town in the southern part of the Indian state of Tripura. The temple is dedicated to the goddess Tripura Sundari, also known as Mata Tripura Sundari or Tripura Devi, and is an important religious and cultural site in Tripura. Option A: Unakoti is known for its ancient rock-cut sculptures and is a different location in Tripura. Option B: Knowai is not the location of the Tripura Sundari Temple. Option D: Agartala is the capital city of Tripura but is not where the Tripura Sundari Temple is located. So, the correct answer is Option C: Udaipur.

question: "Tripura Sundari Temple" constructed in the period of which king?

question: Gunabati Group of Temple is located at _______

question: Pilak is an/a ______

question: In Tripura state, the highest ST population group/tribe is (according 2011 census)
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Tripuris are the largest tribal community in Tripura followed by Reang. Tripuris have first migrated in this territory and could be introduced as aboriginal tribe of Tripura. Numerically as per 2001 Census they are 5,43,843 persons in the State and Tripuris numerically highest in number among all the tribal groups.

question: The Bhils (Tribal group) of Tripura belongs to which tribal group?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Bhils could be identified as one of the Dravidian racial tribes of Western India and belong to Austroloid group of tribes. They speak a language of Dravidian origin. In Tripura total Bhil population is 3,105 as per 2011 Census. This tribe has migrated to Tripura from central India mainly from Bihar and Madhya Pradesh.

question: The main occupation of the Santhal tribal group of Tripura is _______
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Santhals have migrated to Tripura as Tea garden labourers. As per 2001 Census Santals are only 2,151 persons in Tripura. They mainly concentrated in Simna and Mechliban Tea Garden areas of Sadar Sub-division and other places in the State. Their main occupation is to work in Tea garden area.

question: In which year rubber cultivation started in Tripura?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Natural rubber is the most important cash crop in Tripura. Since its introduction in 1963 by the State Forest Department, Tripura has become 2nd largest producer of natural rubber in the country, after Kerala, accounting for about 9% of the total production of India. Tripura also has the second largest rubber growing area in the country, after Kerala.
